1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The major cytokines responsible for cartilage destruction in rheumatoid arthritis are:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: TNF-α and IL-1 drive inflammation and joint destruction in RA.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The first-line drugs for symptomatic relief in rheumatoid arthritis are:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: NSAIDs relieve pain and stiffness but do not stop disease progression.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: NSAIDs in rheumatoid arthritis do NOT:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: NSAIDs are symptomatic agents and do not modify disease progression.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The DMARD of first choice for rheumatoid arthritis is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Methotrexate is the standard first-line DMARD.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Methotrexate inhibits all of the following EXCEPT:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Methotrexate does not inhibit xanthine oxidase.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The typical dosing schedule for methotrexate in rheumatoid arthritis is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Methotrexate is given once weekly in RA.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Which drug increases methotrexate levels?
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Probenecid decreases methotrexate excretion.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: A major dose-dependent adverse effect of chronic methotrexate therapy is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Liver damage is an important long-term toxicity.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Methotrexate is contraindicated in:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Methotrexate is teratogenic and contraindicated in pregnancy.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Azathioprine is converted into:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Azathioprine is a prodrug of 6-mercaptopurine.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The major toxicity of azathioprine is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Bone marrow suppression is a major adverse effect.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Azathioprine should NOT be combined with:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Combination increases toxicity.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The active anti-rheumatic moiety of sulfasalazine is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Sulfapyridine is responsible for anti-rheumatic activity.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Sulfasalazine is commonly used in:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Sulfasalazine is a second-line drug for milder RA.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Hydroxychloroquine is preferred over chloroquine because of:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Hydroxychloroquine has fewer adverse effects.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The most important toxicity of hydroxychloroquine is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Retinopathy is the classic toxicity.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Leflunomide acts by inhibiting:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Leflunomide blocks pyrimidine synthesis.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Leflunomide primarily inhibits proliferation of:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Activated lymphocytes require pyrimidine synthesis.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Combining leflunomide with methotrexate increases the risk of:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Both drugs can cause liver toxicity.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Etanercept works by:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Etanercept is a TNF receptor fusion protein.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Infliximab is best described as a:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Infliximab is a monoclonal antibody against TNF-α.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Adalimumab differs from infliximab because it is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Adalimumab is less immunogenic than infliximab.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The most important adverse effect of biologic DMARDs is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Biologics suppress immunity and increase infection risk.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Anakinra is a:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Anakinra blocks IL-1 signaling.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Abatacept primarily inhibits:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Abatacept blocks T-cell activation.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Rituximab targets:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Rituximab depletes B cells.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Tocilizumab acts by blocking:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Tocilizumab is an IL-6 antagonist.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The hallmark biochemical abnormality in gout is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Gout results from elevated uric acid levels.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Acute gout is caused by deposition of:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Monosodium urate crystals trigger inflammation.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The fastest acting drug for acute gout is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Colchicine rapidly suppresses gouty inflammation.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Colchicine works primarily by binding to:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Tubulin inhibition prevents neutrophil migration.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The dose-limiting toxicity of colchicine is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Severe diarrhea and abdominal cramps limit use.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Probenecid lowers uric acid by:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Probenecid is a uricosuric agent.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Probenecid inhibits renal excretion of:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Probenecid raises levels of several drugs.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: The drug of choice for chronic gout is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Allopurinol is the standard urate-lowering therapy.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Allopurinol acts by inhibiting:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Allopurinol blocks uric acid synthesis.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Allopurinol should NOT be started during:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Starting during an acute attack may worsen symptoms.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Allopurinol increases toxicity of:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Allopurinol inhibits metabolism of both drugs.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Febuxostat differs from allopurinol because it is:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Febuxostat is a non-purine XO inhibitor.

1_antirheumatroid_antigout.q: Pegloticase and rasburicase convert uric acid into:
1_antirheumatroid_antigout.l: Uricase converts uric acid into soluble allantoin.
